methodology: Cross-reference network (MAT.4.4→DEU.8.3=49v — 3rd strongest in entire research; JHN.6.51→JHN.10.10=25v) + patristic reading (13 entries on Jn 6:35; Theophilus AD 184 earliest source; Cassiodorus cross-commentary Ex16:4→Jn6:51; Chrysostom Divinity-bread vs Body-bread; Cyril complete typology manna→12 loaves→12 apostles→Christ; Augustine eucharistic ecclesiology) + topic verification (MANNA, BREAD, COMMUNION) + 5-stage typological chain mapping

evidence: commentaries (13 entries! Chrysostom 2x, Cyril 2x, Lapide, Augustine, Tertullian, Hippolytus, Alcuin, Bede, Cyprian, Hilary, Theophilus)
commentaries (Augustine 'become body of Christ', Bede 'altar of cross', Cyril 2x, Haydock, Tertullian, Theophilus AD 184 'not merely sign but very flesh' — 7 entries)
commentaries (Cassiodorus 'PREFIGURATION — cites Jn 6:51!', Haydock, Peter Chrysologus — 3 entries)
commentaries (Clement 'Word = infinite possession', Haydock, Challoner — 3 entries)

relevance_change.reason: 49 votes to DEU.8.3 (3rd strongest connection in entire research). Verse where Jesus PERSONALLY connects Dt 8:3 to his ministry, creating the OT→NT bridge. Hub connecting ALL John 6 verses (13v each). Cannot be score 1 when it's the structural bridge of the entire typology.

new_refs_added.justification: 13v→MAT.4.4. 5v→JHN.6.51. Central repeated declaration 'I am the bread of life.' Cyril: 'manna was merely image/shadow.' Haydock: 'Christ shows all was FIGURE of spiritual food PRESENT.' 4 catena entries. Topic MANNA.

13v de MAT.4.4. 5v→NEH.9.15, PSA.105.40. Bridge verse: audience cites Ex 16 ('our fathers ate manna') → Jesus responds with Jn 6:35 ('I am the bread of life'). Without it, the typological dialogue is broken.
5v→DEU.8.3. 4v→EXO.16.35. Chadwick cites in Ex 16:15 commentary. Paul reinterprets manna as 'spiritual food' — explicit typological bridge. Connects OT manna → NT sacrament.

candidates_noted.justification: 5v→EXO.16.4, 6v→MAT.4.4. Cassiodorus: 'prefiguration.' Ambrose: 'Word of God nourishes.' Haydock: Eucharist surpasses manna.
6v→JHN.4.14. Closing declaration with explicit contrast: 'fathers ate and DIED; whoever eats this bread will LIVE forever.'
4v de JHN.6.48. Eschatological dimension: 'hidden manna' promised to overcomers.

stages.name: Promise
stages.note: Lapide: 'type of the Eucharist, as Irenaeus teaches'

stages.name: Fulfillment
stages.verse: Jn 6:35→51 (I AM the bread of life → my flesh)
stages.note: Chrysostom: progression from Divinity-bread to Body-bread

earliest_source.quote: Not only the sign of Christ's flesh, but is itself the very flesh of Christ, transmuted by a mystical benediction
earliest_source.significance: Earliest substantial theological declaration in entire research. Pre-Nicene witness to real presence doctrine.

chrysostom_dual_bread.distinction: Two dimensions: (1) Jn 6:35 = Christ's DIVINITY as bread, (2) Jn 6:51 = Christ's FLESH as bread
chrysostom_dual_bread.significance: Tracks the progression within the Bread of Life discourse itself — not one concept but an unfolding revelation

comparison.manna: 1 day supply, corrupted if kept, satisfied physical hunger only, those who ate DIED
comparison.eucharist: Eternal validity, incorruptible, gives eternal life, 'gives grace in this life and glory in the next'
